key_evidence: A ladies' gold ring with an ornate flower design found near the victim's body, appears to have a small hollow compartment in the band
Multiple witnesses report seeing Vivienne and Edmund in an intense private conversation in her office 30 minutes before the opening ceremony
A partially burned shipping manifest found in the gallery's furnace room listing art supplies from a suspicious Belgian supplier
murder_method: Administered arsenic trioxide dissolved in champagne using a hollow ring mechanism that released the poison when she clinked glasses with Edmund during the toast
murder_location: A wealthy art collector and authentication expert, age 52, known for his meticulous research and recent investigations into forgeries flooding the London art market. He was Vivienne's estranged half-brother from their father's first marriage, and they had been competing for their late father's estate
